text: The surgeon performs limited dissection of the upper pole to remove attachments, followed by medial mobilization of the colon. A window is created at the lateral fold of the peritoneum, uplifting the ureter and gonadal vein. Dissection continues upward to the renal hilum, with the lower pole freed from surrounding tissues. Careful dissection is conducted at the adrenal hilum, where the adrenal gland is mobilized superiorly, and the adrenal vein is identified, clipped, and divided.

The surgeon meticulously dissects the mass from the undersurface of the liver. The renal artery is clipped with Hem-o-lok clips and divided. Dense adhesions surrounding the renal vein are removed, and the renal vein is divided between clips. The ureter is clipped and divided. The specimen is placed into a bag and removed through a Pfannenstiel incision.
